nbss_lup_agro_ecological_regions.description: 20 Agro-Ecological Regions (AERs) defined by NBSS & LUP based on soil physiography, landforms, climate, and Length of Growing Period (LGP). Use these codes as the canonical agro_climatic_zones enum.
nbss_lup_agro_ecological_regions.source: NBSS & LUP Publication, 1992; FAO methodology of sequential layering

AER-2.name: Western Plain, Kachchh and part of Kathiawar Peninsula, hot arid ecoregion

AER-4.name: Northern Plain and Central Highlands including Aravallis, hot semi-arid ecoregion

AER-5.name: Central Malwa Highlands, Gujarat Plains and Kathiawar Peninsula, hot semi-arid ecoregion

AER-7.name: Deccan (Telangana) Plateau and Eastern Ghats, hot semi-arid ecoregion

AER-8.name: Eastern Ghats, Tamil Nadu Plateau and Deccan (Karnataka), hot semi-arid to arid ecoregion

AER-10.name: Central Highlands (Malwa, Bundelkhand and Eastern Satpura), hot sub-humid ecoregion

AER-11.name: Eastern Plateau (Chhattisgarh) and Eastern Ghats, hot sub-humid ecoregion

AER-12.name: Eastern (Chhota Nagpur) Plateau and Eastern Ghats, hot sub-humid ecoregion

AER-14.name: Warm sub-humid to humid with inclusion of perhumid ecoregion, with brown forest and podzolic soils

AER-15.name: Bengal and Assam Plains, hot sub-humid (moist) to humid ecoregion

AER-18.name: Hot sub-humid to semi-arid ecoregion with coastal alluvium-derived soils

AER-19.name: West Coast, hot humid perhumid ecoregion

AER-20.name: Islands of Andaman-Nicobar and Lakshadweep, hot humid to perhumid island ecoregion

acwadam_aquifer_types.description: Aquifer classification system used by ACWADAM (Advanced Center for Water Resources Development and Management), India. Used as the canonical aquifer_types enum.

alluvium.description: Unconsolidated sedimentary deposits (sand, gravel, silt, clay) in river valleys, floodplains, and deltas. High storativity and transmissivity. Found in Indo-Gangetic Plains, river deltas, coastal plains.
alluvium.groundwater_behavior: High yield, good recharge response to rainfall, susceptible to over-extraction and contamination

himalayan_and_sub_himalayan.description: Fractured and weathered rocks of the Himalayan orogen plus sub-Himalayan Siwalik sediments. Spring-dominated systems, glacial and snowmelt recharge.
himalayan_and_sub_himalayan.groundwater_behavior: Seasonal springs critical for water supply; climate-sensitive recharge

volcanic.description: Deccan Basalt (trap) and other volcanic formations. Low primary porosity but secondary fracture/vesicle porosity. Thin weathered zone (regolith) stores most groundwater.
volcanic.groundwater_behavior: Low storativity, rainfall-dependent recharge, seasonal wells, overexploitation risk high

sedimentary_soft_rock.description: Sandstone, shale, limestone of Gondwana and Vindhyan formations. Intermediate porosity. Found in MP, Chhattisgarh, Rajasthan parts, NE India.
sedimentary_soft_rock.groundwater_behavior: Moderate yield, aquifer heterogeneity; limestone areas may have karst

sedimentary_hard_rock.description: Compact quartzite, hard sandstone, chert. Low porosity; groundwater limited to fractures and faults.
sedimentary_hard_rock.groundwater_behavior: Low to negligible yield; groundwater in secondary fractures only

crystalline_basement.description: Granite, gneiss, schist, charnockite of the Peninsular shield. Groundwater in weathered zone and fractures. Most of peninsular India.
crystalline_basement.groundwater_behavior: Spatially heterogeneous; shallow dug wells in weathered zone, borewells in fractures; over-exploitation risk very high

rainfall_regime_ranges.description: Rainfall regime classification by annual precipitation band, for use in evidence card context and MWS characterization.

visualization_spec.description: Specification for how to visualize MWS variables individually, in pairs, and in diagnostic panels.

trend_annual_delta_g_mm.note: Negative slope (mm/year) indicates chronic groundwater depletion

annual_well_depth_m.note: User-supplied when available; Y axis inverted so deepening = downward trend

drought_weeks_*.note: Stack no/mild/moderate/severe weeks to show 18-week kharif season

dist_*_km.note: All facility distances in one chart, sorted ascending

annual_precipitation_mm + annual_delta_g.note: Reveals whether delta_g tracks rainfall or diverges due to extraction

annual_precipitation_mm + drought_weeks_.note: Shows whether drought is meteorological or agricultural

cropping_intensity + annual_delta_g_mm.note: Key pair for diagnosing irrigation-driven groundwater depletion

annual_well_depth_m + soge_dev_percent.note: Well depth trend + SOGE gauge side by side for groundwater stress summary

lulc_tree_forest_ha + cd_total_deforesta.note: Show cumulative deforestation as annotated area on forest trend

swb_total_area_ha + annual_precipitation.note: Distinguishes rainfall-driven SWB variation from structural SWB loss

annual_et_mm + annual_runoff_mm + annual.note: Stack ET and Runoff, show P as line above; residual = delta_g. Classic water balance chart.

response_plus_two_causes.description: When diagnosing one response variable against two causal variables, one categorical and one continuous.

example.chart: small_multiples_scatter: one panel per aquifer class, x=cropping_intensity, y=annual_delta_g_mm

water_balance_full.description: P, ET, Runoff, delta_g together across years

default_mws_panel.description: Variables to show by default when a user selects an MWS, before any query is made.
default_mws_panel.rationale: Selected to give a quick orientation to water, land, and socio-economic status of the MWS.

query_triggered_panel_updates.description: When an LLM diagnosis references specific causal pairs, the panel updates to show these visualizations.
